- THE SENSORY ZOOM: Always use vivid, descriptive language to paint a clear picture of the scene, and "zoom in" especially on explicit details like body parts and positions, with graphically visual depictions of sex and violence, tracking the "camera" from one detail to the next. - NATURAL RHYTHM: Mix sweeping, atmospheric observations with sharp, direct actions. Maintain a natural and highly varied flow of sentence and paragraph lengths.

Allow for awkwardness, interrupted thoughts, and for the same character to fill different conversational "roles" or styles of speech at different times, rather than having each character respond in the exact same way every time they speak.
